title: Python API to get YouTube transcript/subtitles. Works for auto-generated subtitl
summary: This repo IS the caption-first approach.  It solves the exact problem the previous two T27 repos missed — extracting YouTube transcripts directly without downloading video or running Whisper.
creator_view: This repo IS the caption-first approach. It solves the exact problem the previous two T27 repos missed — extracting YouTube transcripts directly without downloading video or running Whisper. No API key, no browser, no GPU. Just HTTP requests to YouTube's internal transcript endpoint. 7K+ stars, 24 contributors, CI with coverage, pushed today. This is the production-grade, well-maintained library that should be the FIRST step in any YouTube content extraction pipeline, with Whisper as fallback only when transcripts are unavailable.

candidates.name: youtube-transcript-api as T27 primary extraction layer
candidates.description: pip install. One-line caption fetch. 7K stars, MIT, 100% coverage. First adoption recommendation in series.

candidates.name: Innertube API reverse-engineering approach
candidates.description: Undocumented /youtubei/v1/player API with ANDROID client context. No API key. 8 years maintained.

candidates.name: Context-aware error hierarchy pattern
candidates.description: 15+ exception types with CAUSE_MESSAGE. RequestBlocked.cause adapts based on proxy config. Gold standard error handling.

candidates.name: IP ban workaround operational guide
candidates.description: Production guide to YouTube IP blocks: proxy config, Webshare residential rotation, retries.

candidates.name: Three-layer T27 extraction architecture
candidates.description: Cross-repo synthesis: L1=youtube-transcript-api (instant/free/~80%), L2=pytubefix captions (backup), L3=faster-whisper (GPU fallback). Clear architecture from 3 repos.

candidates.name: Proxy rotation infrastructure
candidates.description: ProxyConfig ABC with Generic + Webshare implementations. Rotating residential IPs, retry-on-429.

candidates.name: Manual vs auto-generated transcript priority
candidates.description: find_transcript() checks manual first. Quality hierarchy baked into architecture.

candidates.name: SRT/WebVTT timestamp formatting
candidates.description: Clean subtitle format generation. SRT (comma) vs WebVTT (dot) separator.

candidates.name: Don't build own transcript fetcher (anti-pattern)
candidates.description: 399 commits over 8 years maintaining YouTube API compatibility. Use the library.

candidates.name: Ignore IP blocking for bulk (anti-pattern)
candidates.description: YouTube blocks bulk requests. Plan proxy infrastructure from the start.

repo.description: Python API to get YouTube transcript/subtitles. Works for auto-generated subtitles. No API key, no headless browser.

QS-02_engagement.detail: 7,257 stars, 743 forks. Dominant in its niche.

QS-05_cicd.detail: CI present (ci.yml), 120 workflow runs. Coveralls integration.

QS-10_community_health.detail: 57/100. Has license, issue templates. Missing CONTRIBUTING, SECURITY.

QS-11_contributing_security.detail: No CONTRIBUTING.md, no SECURITY.md. Has issue templates.

It solves the exact problem the previous two T27 repos missed — extracting YouTube transcripts directly without downloading video or running Whisper. No API key, no browser, no GPU. Just HTTP requests to YouTube's internal transcript endpoint. 7K+ stars, 24 contributors, CI with coverage, pushed today. This is the production-grade, well-maintained library that should be the FIRST step in any YouTube content extraction pipeline, with Whisper as fallback only when transcripts are unavailable.
